about.description: Skiff is a local cross-platform disk cleanup tool for Linux, macOS, and Windows. It checks caches, applications, duplicates, and large files so you can review paths, sizes, and risk before deleting.
agent.confirm: Selected Agent session bodies, thread records, logs, target records, and indexes will be deleted.
agent.empty.description: Agent sessions stored on this machine will appear after scanning.
agent.empty.title: No Agent sessions
agent.failed: Some Agent sessions failed to clean.
agent.searchPlaceholder: Search source, title, directory, or session ID
agent.subtitle: Scan local Agent sessions, filter by source, and precisely delete session records, logs, and indexes.
agent.title: Agent sessions
apps.empty.description: Recognized packages on this system will appear after scanning.
apps.empty.title: No app packages
apps.permission.usuallyNoAdmin: Usually does not require administrator permission
apps.platform.linux.description: Scan installed APT, RPM, Pacman, and Flatpak applications, then call the system package manager after confirmation.
apps.platform.macos.description: Scan .app bundles, Homebrew formulae, and casks, then move to Trash or call brew uninstall after confirmation.
apps.platform.macos.privilege: system applications may require administrator authorization.
apps.platform.macos.scanning: Reading macOS applications and Homebrew lists
apps.platform.windows.description: Scan Windows Uninstall registry entries, then call each application's official uninstall command after confirmation.
apps.platform.windows.privilege: system-level applications may require administrator authorization.
apps.searchPlaceholder: Search package name, app name, or description
apps.title: App packages
duplicates.activity.scanning: Finding duplicates by size, content hash, and byte comparison
duplicates.confirm.description: {count} files will be moved to Trash. Total size: {size}.
duplicates.confirm.more: {count} more files are not expanded here.
duplicates.confirm.recoverable: Files go to the system Trash and can be restored before Trash is emptied.
duplicates.confirm.title: Confirm duplicate files
duplicates.failed: Some files failed to move to Trash.
duplicates.subtitle: Find duplicates by size, content hash, and byte comparison, then move selected files to Trash.
empty.file.description: Files matching the criteria will appear after scanning.
empty.file.title: No results
empty.scan.description: Click the scan button in the toolbar to read the current user's cache directories.
env.confirm.description: Skiff will directly modify this shell's user configuration. Existing files are backed up before saving. Changes only affect newly opened shells unless you run the activation command.
env.confirm.inventoryDescription: {count} environment configuration changes will be saved. Skiff backs up files before writing.
env.confirm.registry: CMD requires writing the current user's Command Processor AutoRun so new CMD windows call the script managed by Skiff.
env.confirm.title: Confirm environment changes
env.discardConfirm: Current changes are not saved. Switching shells will discard them. Continue?
env.inspector.empty: Select an item to review its source, path, and editability.
env.inspector.title: Environment details
env.inventory.subtitle: Review shell configs, Skiff blocks, and Windows user environment entries that write to user-level environment config.
env.inventory.title: Environment inventory
env.invalidKey: Variable names can only contain letters, numbers, and underscores, and cannot start with a number.
env.noShells: No manageable shells on this system.
env.note.imported: Imported from the current environment into the target shell's Skiff-managed block.
env.note.newEntry: New entries are written into the target shell's Skiff-managed block.
env.path.description: PATH entries are appended to the end of the existing PATH in list order.
env.path.empty: No PATH entries managed by Skiff.
env.path.title: PATH
env.registryChanged: Windows user environment was changed. New processes will inherit the updated values.
env.shell.description: Choose the shell config to read and save.
env.shell.title: Shell
env.subtitle: Manage user-level environment variables for zsh, bash, fish, PowerShell, and CMD.
env.table.name: Name
env.variables.description: Variables are written to the selected shell's user config.
env.variables.title: Variables
env.warning.description: Saving directly edits the config file. Open terminals will not update automatically; run the activation command or reopen the shell.
env.warning.registry: CMD also modifies the current user's AutoRun.
env.warning.title: Confirm the impact before saving
history.empty.description: Results appear after a cleanup finishes.
history.empty.title: No records
history.header.subtitle: Recent cleanup, Trash, uninstall, and Agent operation records
history.header.title: Cleanup history
inspector.noTarget: Select an item after scanning to review paths and impact.
inspector.title: Item details
large.activity.scanning: Scanning large files in common user directories
large.confirm.description: {count} files will be moved to Trash. Total size: {size}.
large.confirm.more: {count} more files are not expanded here.
large.confirm.recoverable: Files go to the system Trash and can be restored before Trash is emptied.
large.confirm.title: Confirm large files
large.failed: Some files failed to move to Trash.
large.subtitle: Scan large files in common user directories and move selected files to Trash.
overview.category.subtitle.ready: Review reclaimable space and selection by category
overview.category.title: Cleanup suggestions
overview.hero.caption.cleanable: {count} cleanable items, low-risk items selected by default.
overview.hero.caption.empty: The most recent scan found no reclaimable space. You can scan again later.
overview.hero.caption.initial: Scan system caches, browser caches, and developer tool caches, then confirm before deleting.
settings.advanced.description: When disabled, the sidebar hides Agent and environment variable entries. If an advanced page is already open, its current entry remains visible.
settings.advanced.title: Show advanced feature entries
settings.ai.description: Space analysis sends a directory scan summary to an OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint. The API key is stored in the local settings file.
settings.ai.note: Supports OpenAI, Gemini OpenAI-compatible endpoints, and self-hosted gateways. Only compressed path, size, and file-count summaries are sent.
settings.ai.title: AI analysis endpoint
settings.closeToTray.description: When enabled, the close button hides the window. When disabled, the close button exits the app.
settings.closeToTray.title: Minimize to tray on close
settings.duplicate.description: Duplicate file scanning ignores files smaller than this size.
settings.duplicate.title: Duplicate file threshold
settings.language.description: Choose the language used by the main window and tray menu.
settings.language.title: Language
settings.large.description: Large file scanning only returns files at least this size.
settings.large.title: Large file threshold
settings.scanPaths.description: Leave empty to use built-in default directories. When set, large-file and duplicate scans only read these directories. Paths must stay inside the current user's home directory.
settings.scanPaths.empty: No custom paths are set. Built-in default scan directories are used.
settings.scanPaths.title: File scan paths
settings.subtitle: Adjust file scan paths, large-file and duplicate-file thresholds, interface language, and advanced feature entries.
space.activity.scanning: Recursively measuring directory sizes and largest items
space.ai.emptyDescription: Scan a directory, then call the model for suggestions.
space.ai.initialPrompt: Analyze the current scan result, with priority cleanup targets, risks, and next steps.
space.ai.title: AI analysis
space.directoryDelete.permanentDescripti: Directory "{name}" will be permanently deleted. Estimated release: {size}. This will not go to Trash.
space.directoryDelete.permanentWarning: Permanent delete cannot be restored from system Trash. Use it only when this directory has no business data or only copy.
space.directoryDelete.scope: This operation includes {files} files and {dirs} directories.
space.directoryDelete.trashDescription: Directory "{name}" will be moved to system Trash. Estimated release: {size}.
space.directoryDelete.trashWarning: The directory goes to system Trash and can be restored before Trash is emptied.
space.directoryDelete.typePhrase: Enter the full phrase below to continue:
space.pathDelete.permanentFileDescriptio: File "{name}" will be permanently deleted. Estimated release: {size}. This will not go to Trash.
space.pathDelete.trashFileDescription: File "{name}" will be moved to system Trash. Estimated release: {size}.
space.toolDelete.agentRequested: I created a delete request. Review the path and risk in the confirmation dialog before running it.
space.toolDelete.notInScan: The Agent requested a path outside the current scan result: {path}
space.drop.description: Default scan uses the current user directory
space.drop.title: Drop a folder for space analysis
space.empty.description: Choose or drop a folder to show the usage tree.
space.empty.title: No directory scanned
space.subtitle: Scan any accessible folder, expand the usage tree, and send the largest-item summary to AI for analysis.
space.top.subtitle: Priority context sent to AI
space.top.title: Largest items
space.tree.title: Directory usage
systemStatus.title: System status
table.name: Name
targetList.title: Cleanup items
view.about.description: Learn Skiff's scope and safety boundaries
view.agent.description: Check local Agent sessions and run record storage
view.developer.description: Review installed packages and uninstall after confirmation
view.duplicates.description: Identify duplicate files by content and keep necessary copies
view.environment.description: Manage environment variables and PATH for common shells
view.history.description: Review recent cleanup, Trash, uninstall, and Agent operation records
view.largeFiles.description: Find large files and confirm each deletion
view.overview.description: Central view for disk status, cleanup results, and recent actions
view.settings.description: Adjust scan thresholds and cleanup preferences
view.spaceAnalysis.description: Drop or choose a folder, scan directory usage, and call AI for analysis
